name: Economics Seminar
description: An advanced senior economics seminar — applying micro and macroeconomic theory to contemporary issues, behavioral economics, game theory, economic policy debates, and capstone research.

modules.title: Review: Core Economic Principles
modules.objectives: Review supply and demand, price elasticity, major market structures, and key macroeconomic indicators such as GDP, unemployment, and inflation.
modules.readingNote: Khan Academy AP Macroeconomics — Basic Economics Concepts Review
modules.video2Note: Crash Course Economics #1 — Intro to Economics: Crash Course
modules.assignment: Draw and annotate supply and demand curves for two real markets. Identify one factor shifting each curve and predict the new equilibrium.

modules.title: Behavioral Economics
modules.objectives: Critique the rational-agent assumption; explain cognitive biases including anchoring and loss aversion; analyze nudge theory and prospect theory.
modules.readingNote: Marginal Revolution University — Behavioral Economics Introduction
modules.assignment: Identify two cognitive biases you have observed in your own financial decisions. Propose a nudge that could improve each decision.

modules.title: Game Theory
modules.objectives: Define Nash equilibrium; analyze the Prisoner's Dilemma; apply strategic thinking to business pricing, trade policy, and arms races.
modules.assignment: Construct a 2×2 payoff matrix for a real-world strategic scenario (e.g., OPEC oil production). Identify dominant strategies and the Nash equilibrium.

modules.title: Advanced Microeconomics
modules.objectives: Analyze producer theory, cost structures (fixed vs. variable, marginal cost), profit-maximization rules, and market power in monopoly and oligopoly.
modules.readingNote: Marginal Revolution University — Profit Maximization & Monopoly
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy AP Micro — Economic Profit vs. Accounting Profit
modules.video2Note: Crash Course Economics #24 — Monopolies and Anti-Competitive Markets
modules.assignment: Graph a monopolist's cost and revenue curves. Mark the profit-maximizing output, price, and deadweight loss. Explain why monopoly is inefficient.

modules.title: Labor Economics
modules.objectives: Explain wage determination in competitive and monopsonistic labor markets; evaluate minimum wage effects; analyze unions and human capital investment.
modules.readingNote: Marginal Revolution University — Labor Markets & Wages
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy AP Micro — Labor Market Equilibrium
modules.video2Note: Crash Course Economics #28 — Labor Markets and Minimum Wage
modules.assignment: Research the current federal minimum wage debate. Write a 300-word analysis presenting the strongest arguments on each side, then state your own evidence-based position.

modules.title: Income Inequality & Redistribution
modules.objectives: Measure inequality using the Gini coefficient and Lorenz curve; explain causes of rising inequality; evaluate progressive taxation, transfer payments, and UBI proposals.
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy AP Macro — Income Inequality
modules.video2Note: Crash Course Economics #29 — Income and Wealth Inequality
modules.assignment: Sketch a Lorenz curve for two countries with different Gini coefficients. Identify two policy interventions and explain their expected effects on inequality.

modules.title: Health Economics
modules.objectives: Analyze healthcare as an imperfect market; explain moral hazard and adverse selection in insurance; compare the ACA with single-payer systems.
modules.readingNote: Marginal Revolution University — Health Economics & Market Failures
modules.video2Note: Crash Course Economics #27 — Healthcare Systems & Economics
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Externalities & Market Failure Practice
modules.assignment: Compare the US healthcare system with one universal-coverage system (e.g., Canada, UK). Evaluate each on efficiency, equity, and cost using specific data.

modules.title: Environmental Economics
modules.objectives: Define negative externalities and public goods; compare carbon taxes with cap-and-trade; conduct cost-benefit analysis for environmental regulations.
modules.video2Note: Crash Course Economics #23 — Environmental Economics & Climate Change
modules.assignment: Construct a cost-benefit analysis for a hypothetical carbon tax of $50/ton. Identify at least three benefits and three costs. Conclude whether the policy is justified.

modules.title: Development & Growth Theory
modules.objectives: Apply the Solow growth model; explain the roles of institutions, capital accumulation, and technology; compare East Asian growth miracles with Sub-Saharan African divergence.
modules.readingNote: Marginal Revolution University — Solow Model & Economic Growth
modules.video2Note: Crash Course Economics #16 — Economic Growth & Development
modules.assignment: Compare South Korea and Ghana's economic trajectories since 1960. Identify three institutional or policy factors explaining their divergence. Use GDP-per-capita data.

modules.title: Financial Markets & Crises
modules.objectives: Explain the role of stock markets, bond markets, and financial intermediaries; analyze the causes and consequences of the 2008 financial crisis.
modules.readingNote: Khan Academy — Introduction to the Stock Market
modules.videoNote: Marginal Revolution University — The 2008 Financial Crisis
modules.video2Note: Crash Course Economics #11 — The 2008 Financial Crisis
modules.assignment: Trace the 2008 financial crisis from subprime mortgage origination through bank failures to the real economy. Identify two regulatory gaps that enabled the crisis.

modules.title: Monetary Policy (Advanced)
modules.objectives: Apply the Taylor Rule to evaluate Fed decisions; explain the zero lower bound problem; assess quantitative easing and inflation targeting strategies.
modules.readingNote: Khan Academy AP Macroeconomics — Monetary Policy Review
modules.videoNote: Marginal Revolution University — Monetary Policy Tools
modules.video2Note: Crash Course Economics #29 — Monetary Policy & the Federal Reserve
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy AP Macro — Monetary Policy Practice
modules.assignment: Using Taylor Rule components, evaluate one Fed rate decision from the past five years. Was the rate appropriate given inflation and unemployment at the time? Justify your answer.

modules.title: Fiscal Policy Debates
modules.objectives: Compare Keynesian stimulus with austerity arguments; analyze national debt sustainability using debt-to-GDP ratios; discuss generational equity implications.
modules.readingNote: Khan Academy AP Macroeconomics — Fiscal Policy Review
modules.video2Note: Crash Course Economics #33 — Fiscal Policy & Stimulus
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy AP Macro — Fiscal Policy Practice
modules.assignment: Debate prompt: 'Governments should prioritize deficit reduction over stimulus spending.' Write a 350-word essay arguing one side, using at least two economic concepts or empirical examples.

modules.title: International Economics (Advanced)
modules.objectives: Revisit comparative advantage and trade theory; analyze currency depreciation strategies and global imbalances; evaluate the roles of the IMF and World Bank.
modules.readingNote: Khan Academy AP Macroeconomics — International Trade & Finance Review
modules.videoNote: Marginal Revolution University — International Trade & Comparative Advantage
modules.video2Note: Crash Course Economics #16 — Globalization and Trade
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy AP Macro — International Trade Practice
modules.assignment: Analyze the US-China trade imbalance: use the current account identity to explain it, then evaluate one proposed policy response (tariffs, currency revaluation, or domestic savings policies).

questions.question: Which of the following would cause a rightward shift in the demand curve for electric vehicles?
options: A rise in the price of EVs
A fall in consumer income (if EVs are normal goods)
A government subsidy given to EV buyers
An increase in the cost of lithium batteries
questions.answer: A government subsidy given to EV buyers
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: A government subsidy given to EV buyers.

questions.question: If the price elasticity of demand for a good is −0.3, the good is best described as:
questions.answer: Inelastic
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Inelastic.

questions.question: According to prospect theory, people tend to:
options: Feel losses more intensely than equivalent gains
Prefer certain losses over probabilistic gains
questions.answer: Feel losses more intensely than equivalent gains
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Feel losses more intensely than equivalent gains.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "A nudge, as defined by Thaler and Sunstein, is best described as:" is: An opt-out default that alters choices without restricting options.
questions.answer: True
questions.explanation: True. An opt-out default that alters choices without restricting options is correct.

questions.question: In the Prisoner's Dilemma, the Nash equilibrium outcome is:
options: Both defect, even though mutual cooperation would be better
Both stay silent, avoiding all punishment
questions.answer: Both defect, even though mutual cooperation would be better
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Both defect, even though mutual cooperation would be better.

questions.question: A dominant strategy in game theory is one that:
options: Yields the highest payoff only if the other player cooperates
Is the best response regardless of what the other player does
Always leads to a Pareto-optimal outcome
questions.answer: Is the best response regardless of what the other player does
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Is the best response regardless of what the other player does.

questions.question: A firm should continue producing as long as:
options: Total revenue exceeds total fixed costs
Marginal revenue equals average total cost
Marginal revenue is greater than or equal to marginal cost
Price exceeds average variable cost only
questions.answer: Marginal revenue is greater than or equal to marginal cost
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Marginal revenue is greater than or equal to marginal cost.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which market structure results in the largest deadweight loss relative to perfect competition" is: Monopolistic competition.
questions.answer: False
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Monopoly.

questions.question: Human capital investment theory predicts that workers will accept lower wages when:
options: Jobs are located far from home
Employers offer on-the-job training that raises future earnings
Labor unions negotiate on their behalf
The minimum wage is above equilibrium
questions.answer: Employers offer on-the-job training that raises future earnings
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Employers offer on-the-job training that raises future earnings.

questions.question: A monopsony in the labor market refers to:
options: A single seller of a product
A single buyer of labor, giving it wage-setting power
A union with monopoly bargaining power
A market with many competing employers
questions.answer: A single buyer of labor, giving it wage-setting power
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: A single buyer of labor, giving it wage-setting power.

questions.question: The Gini coefficient is a measure of:
options: Income or wealth inequality within a population
questions.answer: Income or wealth inequality within a population
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Income or wealth inequality within a population.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Moral hazard in health insurance occurs when:" is: People consume more healthcare than they would if paying full price themselves.
questions.answer: True
questions.explanation: True. People consume more healthcare than they would if paying full price themselves is correct.

questions.question: Adverse selection in insurance markets arises because:
options: Insurers have more information than customers
High-risk individuals are more likely to seek insurance, raising average costs
questions.answer: High-risk individuals are more likely to seek insurance, raising average costs
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: High-risk individuals are more likely to seek insurance, raising average costs.

questions.question: Explain the concept of 'anchoring bias' and give one example of how it affects consumer behavior in a real market.
questions.answer: Anchoring bias occurs when individuals rely too heavily on the first piece of information (the 'anchor') when making decisions. For example, a retailer that marks a product 'was $200, now $120' causes consumers to anchor to $200, making $120 feel like a bargain even if the product's true value is lower. This inflates willingness to pay relative to the unanchored benchmark.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Anchoring bias occurs when individuals rely too heavily on the first piece of information (the 'anchor') when making decisions. For example, a retailer that marks a product 'was $200, now $120' causes consumers to anchor to $200, making $120 feel like a bargain even if the product's true value is lower. This inflates willingness to pay relative to the unanchored benchmark..

questions.question: Using the concept of marginal cost and marginal revenue, explain why a monopolist produces less output at a higher price than a perfectly competitive industry would.
questions.answer: In perfect competition, price equals marginal cost (P = MC), ensuring allocative efficiency. A monopolist faces a downward-sloping demand curve, so marginal revenue is below price. The monopolist sets MR = MC, which yields a quantity lower than the competitive output. Because price > MC at this quantity, consumers willing to pay more than the cost of production are priced out, creating deadweight loss.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: In perfect competition, price equals marginal cost (P = MC), ensuring allocative efficiency. A monopolist faces a downward-sloping demand curve, so marginal revenue is below price. The monopolist sets MR = MC, which yields a quantity lower than the competitive output. Because price > MC at this quantity, consumers willing to pay more than the cost of production are priced out, creating deadweight loss..

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "If the government imposes a price ceiling below the equilibrium price, the result is:" is: A surplus.
questions.answer: False
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: A shortage.

questions.question: The anchoring effect is most relevant to which field of economics?
questions.answer: Behavioral economics
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Behavioral economics.

questions.question: In a repeated Prisoner's Dilemma, cooperation is more likely to emerge because:
options: Both players have dominant strategies to cooperate
The game has a definite endpoint known to both players
Players can punish defection in future rounds (tit-for-tat)
questions.answer: Players can punish defection in future rounds (tit-for-tat)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Players can punish defection in future rounds (tit-for-tat).

questions.question: Which cost concept is most relevant when a firm decides whether to shut down in the short run?
questions.answer: Average variable cost
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Average variable cost.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "An increase in the minimum wage is most likely to reduce employment when labor demand is:" is: Elastic.
questions.answer: True
questions.explanation: True. Elastic is correct.

questions.question: A Lorenz curve that bows further from the 45-degree line indicates:
questions.answer: Higher inequality
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Higher inequality.

questions.question: A carbon tax corrects the market failure caused by carbon emissions by:
options: Making producers internalize the external cost of pollution
Setting a quota on total emissions permits
questions.answer: Making producers internalize the external cost of pollution
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Making producers internalize the external cost of pollution.

questions.question: In the Solow growth model, an economy reaches a steady state when:
options: GDP grows at the fastest possible rate
Investment exactly offsets depreciation of the capital stock
questions.answer: Investment exactly offsets depreciation of the capital stock
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Investment exactly offsets depreciation of the capital stock.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "The 2008 financial crisis was primarily triggered by:" is: A sudden spike in oil prices.
questions.answer: False
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Defaults on subprime mortgages and the collapse of mortgage-backed securities.

questions.question: Quantitative easing (QE) is a monetary policy tool that involves:
options: The government cutting taxes to boost spending
Raising the federal funds rate to curb inflation
The central bank buying long-term assets to inject money into the economy
Setting a price floor on government bonds
questions.answer: The central bank buying long-term assets to inject money into the economy
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: The central bank buying long-term assets to inject money into the economy.

questions.question: The Taylor Rule suggests the Fed should raise interest rates when:
options: Inflation is below target and unemployment is above the natural rate
Inflation is above target or the output gap is positive
Quantitative easing has expanded the money supply
The federal deficit exceeds 3% of GDP
questions.answer: Inflation is above target or the output gap is positive
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Inflation is above target or the output gap is positive.

questions.question: Keynesian economists argue that during a severe recession, the government should:
options: Cut spending to restore business confidence
Raise interest rates to prevent inflation
Increase spending or cut taxes to stimulate aggregate demand
Devalue the currency to boost exports
questions.answer: Increase spending or cut taxes to stimulate aggregate demand
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Increase spending or cut taxes to stimulate aggregate demand.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Comparative advantage in international trade means a country should specialize in goods:" is: That it can produce at a lower opportunity cost than its trading partners.
questions.answer: True
questions.explanation: True. That it can produce at a lower opportunity cost than its trading partners is correct.

questions.question: Currency depreciation tends to:
options: Make a country's exports more expensive and imports cheaper
Make a country's exports cheaper and imports more expensive
Have no effect on the trade balance
questions.answer: Make a country's exports cheaper and imports more expensive
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Make a country's exports cheaper and imports more expensive.

questions.question: A positive externality from education justifies government subsidies because:
options: Individuals underinvest, leading to less education than is socially optimal
questions.answer: Individuals underinvest, leading to less education than is socially optimal
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Individuals underinvest, leading to less education than is socially optimal.

questions.question: The zero lower bound problem occurs when:
options: Fiscal policy cannot increase government spending further
The central bank cannot lower nominal interest rates below zero, limiting expansionary monetary policy
Quantitative easing raises long-term interest rates
The money multiplier falls to zero
questions.answer: The central bank cannot lower nominal interest rates below zero, limiting expansionary monetary policy
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: The central bank cannot lower nominal interest rates below zero, limiting expansionary monetary policy.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "According to the IMF's mandate, it primarily aims to:" is: Finance infrastructure projects in developing countries.
questions.answer: False
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Promote international monetary stability and serve as a lender of last resort to countries in crisis.

questions.question: Explain the difference between a carbon tax and a cap-and-trade system. Under what conditions might each be preferable as a climate policy?
questions.answer: A carbon tax sets a price per ton of CO₂, letting the quantity of emissions adjust. A cap-and-trade system sets a fixed quantity of permitted emissions and lets price adjust through tradeable permits. A carbon tax is preferable when policymakers have good information about the marginal cost of abatement and want price certainty to guide investment. Cap-and-trade is preferable when hitting a precise emissions target is the priority, since the cap guarantees a ceiling on total emissions regardless of economic conditions.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: A carbon tax sets a price per ton of CO₂, letting the quantity of emissions adjust. A cap-and-trade system sets a fixed quantity of permitted emissions and lets price adjust through tradeable permits. A carbon tax is preferable when policymakers have good information about the marginal cost of abatement and want price certainty to guide investment. Cap-and-trade is preferable when hitting a precise emissions target is the priority, since the cap guarantees a ceiling on total emissions regardless of economic conditions..

questions.question: Describe two reasons why GDP per capita may be a poor measure of a country's standard of living, and suggest one alternative or supplemental indicator.
questions.answer: First, GDP per capita ignores distribution: a country with high average income may have extreme inequality, leaving most citizens worse off than the average suggests. Second, GDP omits non-market production such as unpaid care work and environmental degradation, overstating well-being when growth comes at ecological cost. A supplemental measure is the Human Development Index (HDI), which incorporates life expectancy and education alongside income, providing a more comprehensive picture of living standards.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: First, GDP per capita ignores distribution: a country with high average income may have extreme inequality, leaving most citizens worse off than the average suggests. Second, GDP omits non-market production such as unpaid care work and environmental degradation, overstating well-being when growth comes at ecological cost. A supplemental measure is the Human Development Index (HDI), which incorporates life expectancy and education alongside income, providing a more comprehensive picture of living standards..

questions.question: In your capstone policy brief, you recommended a specific economic policy. Summarize your recommendation in 3–4 sentences and identify one key trade-off your proposal involves.
questions.answer: This is an open-response question graded on the quality of the student's individual policy brief recommendation. A full-credit response should clearly state the policy, connect it to at least one economic concept studied in the course, and identify a genuine trade-off — for example, a carbon tax raises revenue and reduces emissions but imposes regressive costs on lower-income households unless offset by dividend rebates.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: This is an open-response question graded on the quality of the student's individual policy brief recommendation. A full-credit response should clearly state the policy, connect it to at least one economic concept studied in the course, and identify a genuine trade-off — for example, a carbon tax raises revenue and reduces emissions but imposes regressive costs on lower-income households unless offset by dividend rebates..

quizQuestions.question: The law of demand states that, all else equal, as price increases:
quizQuestions.answer: Quantity demanded decreases
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Quantity demanded decreases.

quizQuestions.question: Which macroeconomic indicator measures the total market value of all final goods and services produced within a country in a year?
quizQuestions.answer: GDP
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: GDP.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "The law of demand states that, all else equal, as price increases:" is: Quantity demanded decreases.
quizQuestions.answer: True
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Quantity demanded decreases is correct.

quizQuestions.question: The traditional economic assumption that individuals always choose to maximize utility is called:
quizQuestions.answer: The rational agent assumption
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: The rational agent assumption.

quizQuestions.question: Anchoring bias occurs when people:
options: Rely too heavily on the first piece of information encountered
quizQuestions.answer: Rely too heavily on the first piece of information encountered
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Rely too heavily on the first piece of information encountered.

quizQuestions.question: Prospect theory, developed by Kahneman and Tversky, shows that people:
options: Are indifferent between losses and gains of the same size
Value gains more than losses of equivalent magnitude
Value losses more than gains of equivalent magnitude
Always make optimal decisions under risk
quizQuestions.answer: Value losses more than gains of equivalent magnitude
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Value losses more than gains of equivalent magnitude.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "The traditional economic assumption that individuals always choose to maximize utility is called:" is: The rational agent assumption.
quizQuestions.answer: True
quizQuestions.explanation: True. The rational agent assumption is correct.

quizQuestions.question: A Nash equilibrium is a situation in which:
options: Each player's strategy is the best response to the other player's strategy
quizQuestions.answer: Each player's strategy is the best response to the other player's strategy
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Each player's strategy is the best response to the other player's strategy.

quizQuestions.question: In the classic Prisoner's Dilemma, two players who cannot communicate will:
quizQuestions.answer: Both defect
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Both defect.

quizQuestions.question: Which real-world scenario most closely resembles a Prisoner's Dilemma?
options: Two firms choosing to advertise or not when both advertising hurts total industry profit
A firm setting its own price in a perfectly competitive market
A government deciding on its national budget
quizQuestions.answer: Two firms choosing to advertise or not when both advertising hurts total industry profit
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Two firms choosing to advertise or not when both advertising hurts total industry profit.

quizQuestions.question: Marginal cost is defined as:
options: Total cost divided by total output
The change in total cost from producing one additional unit
Fixed cost per unit of output
The difference between price and average variable cost
quizQuestions.answer: The change in total cost from producing one additional unit
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: The change in total cost from producing one additional unit.

quizQuestions.question: A firm in a perfectly competitive market is a 'price taker' because:
options: It has no market power and must accept the market price
quizQuestions.answer: It has no market power and must accept the market price
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: It has no market power and must accept the market price.

quizQuestions.question: Price discrimination by a monopolist involves:
options: Charging one uniform price to all consumers
Charging different prices to different consumer groups based on willingness to pay
Lowering price below marginal cost to eliminate rivals
Colluding with other firms on pricing
quizQuestions.answer: Charging different prices to different consumer groups based on willingness to pay
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Charging different prices to different consumer groups based on willingness to pay.

quizQuestions.question: Economies of scale occur when:
options: Average total cost rises as output increases
Average total cost falls as output increases
Marginal cost exceeds average total cost
quizQuestions.answer: Average total cost falls as output increases
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Average total cost falls as output increases.

quizQuestions.question: The equilibrium wage in a competitive labor market is determined by:
options: The intersection of labor supply and labor demand
quizQuestions.answer: The intersection of labor supply and labor demand
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: The intersection of labor supply and labor demand.

quizQuestions.question: Human capital refers to:
options: The stock of skills, knowledge, and education embodied in workers
quizQuestions.answer: The stock of skills, knowledge, and education embodied in workers
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: The stock of skills, knowledge, and education embodied in workers.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "The equilibrium wage in a competitive labor market is determined by:" is: The intersection of labor supply and labor demand.
quizQuestions.answer: True
quizQuestions.explanation: True. The intersection of labor supply and labor demand is correct.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Human capital refers to:" is: Physical machinery owned by workers.
quizQuestions.answer: False
quizQuestions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: The stock of skills, knowledge, and education embodied in workers.

quizQuestions.question: A Gini coefficient of 0 represents:
quizQuestions.answer: Perfect equality of income
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Perfect equality of income.

quizQuestions.question: A progressive income tax system is one in which:
options: All taxpayers pay the same dollar amount
Higher-income earners pay a lower percentage of income in taxes
Higher-income earners pay a higher percentage of income in taxes
Tax rates decrease as income rises
quizQuestions.answer: Higher-income earners pay a higher percentage of income in taxes
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Higher-income earners pay a higher percentage of income in taxes.

quizQuestions.question: Transfer payments such as unemployment insurance and food stamps are designed to:
quizQuestions.answer: Redistribute income to lower-income households
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Redistribute income to lower-income households.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "A Gini coefficient of 0 represents:" is: Perfect equality of income.
quizQuestions.answer: True
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Perfect equality of income is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Adverse selection in insurance refers to:
options: High-risk individuals being more likely to seek insurance, driving up premiums
Governments mandating coverage for all citizens
quizQuestions.answer: High-risk individuals being more likely to seek insurance, driving up premiums
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: High-risk individuals being more likely to seek insurance, driving up premiums.

quizQuestions.question: Moral hazard in health insurance occurs when insured individuals:
options: Lie about their health status to get coverage
Use more healthcare than they would if they paid full price
quizQuestions.answer: Use more healthcare than they would if they paid full price
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Use more healthcare than they would if they paid full price.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Adverse selection in insurance refers to:" is: High-risk individuals being more likely to seek insurance, driving up premiums.
quizQuestions.answer: True
quizQuestions.explanation: True. High-risk individuals being more likely to seek insurance, driving up premiums is correct.

quizQuestions.question: A negative externality exists when:
options: A firm's production benefits third parties who don't pay
A firm's production imposes costs on third parties who are not compensated
A good is excludable and rival
quizQuestions.answer: A firm's production imposes costs on third parties who are not compensated
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: A firm's production imposes costs on third parties who are not compensated.

quizQuestions.question: A carbon tax set equal to the social cost of carbon achieves efficiency by:
options: Making the market price reflect both private and external costs
Allowing unlimited emissions with a permit system
quizQuestions.answer: Making the market price reflect both private and external costs
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Making the market price reflect both private and external costs.

quizQuestions.question: Under a cap-and-trade system, the total quantity of emissions is:
options: Fixed by the cap; firms buy and sell permits
Set by each individual firm voluntarily
Determined by international negotiation each year
quizQuestions.answer: Fixed by the cap; firms buy and sell permits
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Fixed by the cap; firms buy and sell permits.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "A negative externality exists when:" is: A firm's production imposes costs on third parties who are not compensated.
quizQuestions.answer: True
quizQuestions.explanation: True. A firm's production imposes costs on third parties who are not compensated is correct.

quizQuestions.question: In the Solow growth model, the steady state is reached when:
options: Investment equals depreciation of the capital stock
quizQuestions.answer: Investment equals depreciation of the capital stock
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Investment equals depreciation of the capital stock.

quizQuestions.question: Conditional convergence in growth theory predicts that poorer countries will grow faster than richer ones, assuming they have:
options: Similar institutions, savings rates, and policies
quizQuestions.answer: Similar institutions, savings rates, and policies
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Similar institutions, savings rates, and policies.

quizQuestions.question: The 'East Asian miracle' (South Korea, Taiwan, Singapore) is often attributed to:
options: High savings rates, export-led growth, and strong institutions
Foreign aid from the United States
Low tariff barriers from the beginning
quizQuestions.answer: High savings rates, export-led growth, and strong institutions
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: High savings rates, export-led growth, and strong institutions.

quizQuestions.question: Financial intermediaries such as banks create value primarily by:
options: Channeling savings from depositors to productive borrowers
Setting interest rates for the economy
quizQuestions.answer: Channeling savings from depositors to productive borrowers
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Channeling savings from depositors to productive borrowers.

quizQuestions.question: A mortgage-backed security (MBS) is:
options: A government bond backed by tax revenue
A financial instrument that bundles mortgage loans and sells claims on their cash flows
A government subsidy for first-time home buyers
quizQuestions.answer: A financial instrument that bundles mortgage loans and sells claims on their cash flows
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: A financial instrument that bundles mortgage loans and sells claims on their cash flows.

quizQuestions.question: The 2008 financial crisis spread from housing markets to the broader economy mainly through:
options: A sudden increase in government spending
Losses on mortgage-backed securities that weakened banks and froze credit markets
A dramatic fall in oil prices
quizQuestions.answer: Losses on mortgage-backed securities that weakened banks and froze credit markets
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Losses on mortgage-backed securities that weakened banks and froze credit markets.

quizQuestions.question: The Dodd-Frank Act (2010) was designed to:
options: Stimulate consumer spending after the recession
Regulate financial markets and reduce systemic risk after the 2008 crisis
quizQuestions.answer: Regulate financial markets and reduce systemic risk after the 2008 crisis
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Regulate financial markets and reduce systemic risk after the 2008 crisis.

quizQuestions.question: The federal funds rate is:
options: The rate at which banks lend to households
The overnight interest rate banks charge each other for reserve lending, set as a target by the Fed
The interest rate on 10-year Treasury bonds
The rate the Fed charges banks to borrow from the discount window
quizQuestions.answer: The overnight interest rate banks charge each other for reserve lending, set as a target by the Fed
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: The overnight interest rate banks charge each other for reserve lending, set as a target by the Fed.

quizQuestions.question: The Taylor Rule prescribes that central banks should raise interest rates when:
options: Unemployment rises above the natural rate
Inflation exceeds its target or the output gap is positive
The money supply grows too slowly
quizQuestions.answer: Inflation exceeds its target or the output gap is positive
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Inflation exceeds its target or the output gap is positive.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "The federal funds rate is:" is: The overnight interest rate banks charge each other for reserve lending, set as a target by the Fed.
quizQuestions.answer: True
quizQuestions.explanation: True. The overnight interest rate banks charge each other for reserve lending, set as a target by the Fed is correct.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "The Taylor Rule prescribes that central banks should raise interest rates when:" is: Unemployment rises above the natural rate.
quizQuestions.answer: False
quizQuestions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Inflation exceeds its target or the output gap is positive.

quizQuestions.question: Keynesian economists argue that during a recession, the government should:
options: Cut spending and raise taxes to balance the budget
Increase spending or reduce taxes to boost aggregate demand
Allow automatic stabilizers to work without discretionary policy
Raise interest rates to restore confidence
quizQuestions.answer: Increase spending or reduce taxes to boost aggregate demand
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Increase spending or reduce taxes to boost aggregate demand.

quizQuestions.question: The fiscal multiplier measures:
options: The ratio of government debt to GDP
How much GDP changes for each dollar of government spending
The tax rate applied to the highest income bracket
The ratio of federal revenue to federal expenditure
quizQuestions.answer: How much GDP changes for each dollar of government spending
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: How much GDP changes for each dollar of government spending.

quizQuestions.question: Austerity policies refer to:
options: Government spending increases funded by new taxes
Cuts in government spending and/or tax increases to reduce deficits
Central bank purchases of government bonds
quizQuestions.answer: Cuts in government spending and/or tax increases to reduce deficits
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Cuts in government spending and/or tax increases to reduce deficits.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Keynesian economists argue that during a recession, the government should:" is: Increase spending or reduce taxes to boost aggregate demand.
quizQuestions.answer: True
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Increase spending or reduce taxes to boost aggregate demand is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Comparative advantage means a country should export goods for which it has:
options: An absolute cost advantage over all trading partners
The lowest opportunity cost of production relative to other goods
quizQuestions.answer: The lowest opportunity cost of production relative to other goods
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: The lowest opportunity cost of production relative to other goods.

quizQuestions.question: A current account deficit means a country:
options: Exports more goods and services than it imports
Imports more goods and services than it exports
Holds more foreign currency reserves than it needs
quizQuestions.answer: Imports more goods and services than it exports
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Imports more goods and services than it exports.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Comparative advantage means a country should export goods for which it has:" is: The lowest opportunity cost of production relative to other goods.
quizQuestions.answer: True
quizQuestions.explanation: True. The lowest opportunity cost of production relative to other goods is correct.
